intro: The clearest split is that Novu has a free tier while Airship does not. Airship is Mobile engagement platform with push notifications, in-app messaging, SMS, email, and real-time data streaming; Novu is Open-source notification infrastructure with multi-channel support, in-app feed, user preferences, and self-hostable backend. The data points Airship at mobile app engagement and user lifecycle messaging, and Novu at mobile app engagement and user lifecycle messaging. Pricing: Airship is subscription from $9; Novu is freemium, free tier. Lock-in is low for Airship and low for Novu, with DX at 4/5 vs 5/5 and transparency at 2/5 vs 5/5. On listed strengths, Airship emphasises mobile app engagement and user lifecycle messaging, and Novu emphasises mobile app engagement and user lifecycle messaging. Both offer an enterprise tier for larger rollouts. Honest trade-off: neither fits web-only products without mobile, so if that is your context, look elsewhere; otherwise pick on the pivotal factor above and the DX score that matters most to your team.
quick_take: Airship is for mobile; Novu is for mobile; decide based on whether a free tier matters.
verdict_a: Choose Airship if starting on a paid subscription upfront is acceptable and your workload matches mobile. Skip it if your context is web-only products without mobile.
verdict_b: Choose Novu if you want to start on a free tier before paying and your workload matches mobile. Skip it if your context is web-only products without mobile.

faq.a: Listed entry pricing is $9 for Airship and $0 for Novu. Novu offers a free tier while Airship does not, so initial cost favors Novu. Total cost at scale depends on usage and plan details that are not in this dataset, so confirm with each vendor.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Airship to Novu?
faq.a: Airship is tagged with low lock-in (audiences and channels bound to airship data model). Novu is tagged with low lock-in (oss notification infrastructure, self-hostable). Both sides are low lock-in, so a migration is feasible but still needs planning around data export and SDK rewrites.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, Airship or Novu?
faq.a: The dataset rates Novu at 5/5 for developer experience and Airship at 4/5, so Novu scores higher. That score reflects overall polish rather than any single integration, so trial both against your stack before committing.

faq.q: Is Novu a good alternative to Airship?
faq.a: Yes for teams whose workload matches mobile, since both list the same fit. Novu is flagged as not for web-only products without mobile, so confirm your context does not fall there. Note that moving from Airship to Novu means gaining access to a free tier.
